Keeping this mind and wanting to support small / local business, I selected Window King over big box and 3 other local contractors. Window King offer Onka products, which is a solid mid tier local brand.

Sales process: I called Window King in April,  and they were able to come to my place within a week.  Nicholas was very detail in explaining the differences between Onka series 400 vs 500, demo how the window worked.  Before he left, he was able to share price quote over email. Since everything was on a template, all details were clearly stated on the quote.  No guessing game.

Order / Scheduling: I place the order in April and the entire order was not installed until July.  I had both windows and patio door replaced. I opt to one day installation so had to wait longer for the door.  Throughout the process, Lisa was very helpful and transparent with order status and scheduling.

Installation: There was a mixed up with the order and the installer didn't have everything needed. It was supposed to be one day installation and it ended up to be 2 days. Lisa was kind enough to reschedule my installing the next day so it can be done before the rain. The installation process wasn't perfect but they were responsive and quick to fix issues. From the very first point of contact with Window World, I was struck by the exceptional level of service they provided. The sales agent, Ken S., was not only knowledgeable but also keenly attentive, taking the time to grasp my specific needs and preferences. He expertly navigated me through an array of window styles and door options, sharing comprehensive details about energy efficiency, materials, and design choices. This bespoke approach instilled a sense of confidence in my decisions, making the entire process feel personalized and focused on my satisfaction.

After placing my order, Window World promptly informed me about the anticipated installation timeline. I genuinely appreciated the regular updates they provided, which kept me well-informed about the approximate date for installation—it was reassuring to see that everything was executed right on schedule. This transparent communication allowed me to prepare my home effectively and coordinate my schedule around the installation.

On the day of the installation, the crew arrived punctually at the agreed-upon time, exuding professionalism from the moment they set foot on my property. They were fully equipped and ready to tackle the tasks at hand. I was particularly taken by their meticulous approach; they carefully dismantled the old windows and sliding door, ensuring that my home sustained no damage throughout the process.

One aspect that particularly left an impression on me was their unwavering dedication to cleanliness. After completing the installation, the crew made a concerted effort to leave my home in pristine condition. Each of my belongings was treated with the utmost care, and they took the time to clean up every scrap and dust particle, ensuring that their work left no trace behind—a testament to their professionalism.

If you're considering enhancing your home with a new sliding door, basement windows, or any other window replacements, I wholeheartedly recommend Window World. Their pricing stands out as incredibly competitive compared to many other window companies, all while offering high-quality products that are backed by a transferable lifetime warranty. This warranty is a remarkable selling point, as it can be transferred to new homeowners, adding immense value if you ever decide to sell your property.

Moreover, their customer service is truly exceptional; any questions or concerns I had were addressed promptly and courteously, showing their commitment to client satisfaction. Overall, my experience was thoroughly rewarding, and I wouldn't hesitate to recommend Window World to anyone in need of window or door replacements. Keep up the fantastic work, Window World! I eagerly look forward to returning for my next home improvement project.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBZjhLHckKe9g46T8E__KWA&fid=0x0:0x7c3022b621b8dd55"],"When":"2025-9-12"},{"Name":"Sean Reeves","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUsfb_b8tfHkhvrpyAwI_yypSQ3RDERlKw14bsvgGh41vcoWT_HVA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Excellent experience from the sales team to customer service to installers. [{"Name":"Mike Zafonte","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXEqtYzyyJl7oJdc2PIwZRXg3oiwTZycGk3TLRkOhjbD4bwKOI=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I was recently in need of replacing 3 triangular picture windows that were original to my 36-year-old home in Monmouth county, NJ. The seals were broken and moisture had seeped in.  The windows were this way for YEARS. Most local contractors wanted no part of the job. When I called ARDMOR to explain the situation, they were receptive, professional and timely. Everyone I dealt with was courteous and understanding, and they provided realistic estimates of cost and time to produce the windows and schedule installation. They took on the job, and completed the installation of the custom-built windows today.  The transformation is dramatic!  I couldn't be happier, and can't recommend them highly enough! THANK YOU to everyone at ARDMOR for a great job!!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC7lNCMhAE&fid=0x0:0x2139697a1e97dec4"],"When":"2024-8-12"},{"Name":"Christopher Black","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV2bv33luN1RqpqsSR5dNnaakMzDjzky-xhkoM46uBUmTXJ97Bu=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I recently hired Ardmore Windows and Doors to replace 18 windows in my home, and while the sales process started off strong, the rest of the experience was incredibly disappointing.

To begin with, the sales representative was punctual, knowledgeable, and thorough. He clearly explained all the window options and helped us choose the style we wanted. Unfortunately, that is where the professionalism ended.

One of the most alarming issues occurred during installation: a window was either dropped or thrown from the second floor onto my front lawn, where it shattered. The crew made no attempt to clean it up. I only discovered the shards the next day while outside barefoot with my toddler. I spent hours picking up tiny pieces of glass myself. When I raised this with the project manager, he didn’t apologize or show any concern—he simply offered $100 off for my “time.”

Beyond that, the overall project management and execution were riddled with issues. I had to review the order sheet at least ten times because the company repeatedly made mistakes—wrong quantities, wrong styles, wrong hardware. Even after all of that, the final order was still incorrect. I requested that the new windows match the existing styles (e.g., 6 over 1, 8 over 1), yet this was ignored, and we only realized the error during installation when it was too late to correct.

They also installed two top sashes in one window and acted as if this was normal. Their explanation was that “owners ask for ridiculous things sometimes,” so they didn’t question it. This was the only window of the 18 installed incorrectly, yet I was the one who had to push to have it acknowledged.

The hardware situation was equally unprofessional. They installed mismatched pieces—gold locks with nickel lifts—because the hardware we selected was “out of stock,” but they never told us. When we asked for replacements, they simply handed me a catalog and told me to choose new ones, offering no guidance, even though only one alternative actually worked with the holes they had already drilled.

Another major issue involved the window screens. I requested screens for all windows in the original estimate, and this was clearly documented. Despite that, no screens were installed. Their excuse was that “homeowners don’t often want to open their windows.” After the mistake was pointed out, they installed makeshift screens that don’t fit properly and are extremely difficult to remove and replace—clearly not the correct screens for the windows we purchased.

The job, which was supposed to take a few days, dragged on for weeks due to unfinished work, installation errors, and repeated reorders caused by their own mistakes. Some of the work is still unfinished.

I would not recommend Ardmore Windows and Doors to anyone. Unless you want to act as your own project manager, clean up after the crew, and still end up with results that don’t match what you paid tens of thousands of dollars for, look elsewhere.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Amanda Paul","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKP1Jj8wPNbABECEcZrlgd59UKVcCafPZWOZrC1TBWiTWkOPQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"We had a door installed by Ardmor 2 years ago. The team did not get the installation correct (there were gaps between the seal and the door frame) but after a few visits they fixed it. Recently, we have started having water come under our door. We have contacted Ardmor numerous times to have someone come take a look at the door. We called at least 5 times, everytime with a promise that the scheduler would call us back, yet the scheduler never did. We finally got a call and had someone come out. He is stumped as to what the problem is. He peeled back the quarter round under the door, but did not have a nail gun to reattach it. He took photos to send to his manager, with a promise that the manger would be in touch. A week later and we still have not received a call. We followed up today and again were promised we’d receive a call, and again we did not.

We are now left with a leaky door that continues to damage our hardwood floor, and a quarter round that is loose.

I would not recommend Ardmor. The inability to get ahold of anyone or have customer service accountability has been incredibly frustrating","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBQcO_3fz2GVJqyNbV7HR4Q&fid=0x0:0x2139697a1e97dec4"],"When":"2025-7-3"},{"Name":"Abhay Jha","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIEyWvNZurdTK2WbZ5VRgdASy9c-TlfP4HiOqphs45tn60xbg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Replaced glass in Bow windows: really awesome job. [{"Name":"Tia Sanders (Hopechest Consultations)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWtcbxZOZUB-gRZqJ-pC03gDFlS8otzQDADKgvQLzUAc0DpNYHh=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Go with another company. SAVE YOURSELF THE TROUBLE. It has taken me a year to calm down so I can write this review.This was an absolute annoying and difficult process from start to finish. To start the salesman was  aggressive, demeaning to me and talked bad about all other companies. He tried to overtalk me, offered me deals then took them back at the end,  spoke of one warranty and then said I misunderstood him when he gave me another one in writing. I was so frustrated after hours of the presentation and the rudeness I declined the offer and asked him to leave because he was being deceptive as if I was not an intelligent person who can read what was given to me versus what was said. He eventually called his supervisor after he realized he offered me a warranty that didn't line up with the windows I bought. It was so bad the supervisor gave me a tremendous deal on a supoosedly better quality window matching another quote so against my better judgment I signed the contract. He then took my other quotes without my permission and I had to demand they be returned.  Less than a month later that salesman no longer worked there. I thought it was an overzealous salesman that was a terrible experience but boy was I wrong. They measured the windows wrong so a month later had to come back to remeasure. Then the window that was made was wrong so they had to order a new one.  The people who came to install cracked my tree limbs with the truck. Then put the wrong windows in the wrong place. I had to point out the gaps in the windowseal. They then had to take the windows out and move them to the right location switching upstairs for downstairs. They left chipped wall under the seal, cleaned up and went outside to work on the outside. I had to point it out because he did not notice it. I had to ask him to finish it. He then went back out and brought more supplies in to finish and then had to clean up again so I know he wasn't coming back the first time as the supervisor tried to suggest when I called to report  the entire mess. The caulk on the outside was not smooth I had to have them redo it.  Every part of this process was stressful.  I was quoted at 8 week for job completion. From the signing of the contract to being told that they would rush it for the errors  to saying it was delayed for the holidays it took 4 months to complete it (Oct start to Feb end). The job took twice as long as quoted. Almost everyone I spoke with to complain throughout tried to gaslight me as if I wasn't having these experiences. I had to email complaints and call constantly. The people I emailed never responded directly even though I was given their info for any concerns. However they delegated to a supervisor who did return my calls. Even the part about the gift card for just having a consultation took multiple calls to get.The only thing positive is that the windows look nice however I'm pretty sure they are breezy. I feel cold when I stand closely. They either didn't insulate them properly or the windows aren't that great. Please do not use them. Spend the money elsewhere no matter what discount price they give you. It's not worth the headache. I hope the windows last because  I dread having to use the warranty and have to deal with them again.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB0ETq-_0LRyviA0BpnHYNc&fid=0x0:0xa7ea51b4b9f841e9"],"When":"2026-1-21"},{"Name":"Rimsha Rashid","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIqwolSy-dZmIh_-sx-XFFE1hWsOQuPxhDGdFIJzW8LjAU76A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Update 2:
Its been over a month issue still hasn't been solved. Windows Nation called today and said I need to make remaining payment and that's only when they would come and fix the issue. I firmly said NO, work needs to be done first because if work is not being done without final payment why should I believe that it would be done after clearing payment. Representative said okay our attorney will call you shortly to take me to the court.

Before we even touched them, we could see four windows with cracks and broken pieces on the outer flanges, one with cracked interior trim, and another with a cracked outer frame.

​Dealing with their warranty department is an exercise in futility. We were stuck in a loop for over a month, constantly being transferred and getting the run-around. When they finally offered a 'resolution,' it was insulting. They are only replacing the outer frame of one window and expecting us to DIY the repair by popping out the interior windows to place in the new frame.

​The staff treated this solution like they were doing me a massive favor and even refused to deliver the replacement directly to my home. The service was fast, professional, and the exact price they quoted over the phone. I would absolutely hire them again.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBYtCvjbSJtikqHODgeYDEI&fid=0x0:0x76dcba4db9e18597"],"When":"2026-2-7"},{"Name":"Mr Brooklyn Resident","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLhXlVmSw0K1GuetW8nJOraXBeK70KhJnv4iNraWDGM1GedwQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Last winter I used window repair to redo all the caulk on my windows on the exterior. I have many floor to ceiling windows and they were leaking air.  If this was that review it would be 4 stars with me being very satisfied with the work and feeling like the price was a little high. I had a good experience overall and decided to use them again.

Fast forward to Dec 2025. My french doors are still leaking air which is unrelated to the prior work, and I call them up again. I told them I’m pretty sure it’s a simple caulk job and I just need them to come and make it look professional. They call me up and tell me a tall tale about gaskets and how difficult my doors are and quote me $3400 for custom gaskets. I I am not the window guy expert  and chose to trust them. It sounded complicated I had no reason not to. With some hindsight they were just making up bs to inflate the price. They made a big deal about “taking a sample” and showed up eight hours late to do so only after I harassed them to find out if anyone was coming. I think it was part of the theater in retrospect. Sow is attached for anyone interested

On the day of, their tech shows up and does a sloppy job caulking all my windows. They charge me $3200 for 1.5 hours of work. I don’t think I’ve ever been this ripped off in my entire life. I’ve tried reaching out, only to be ignored. Anyone I tell how much I paid is floored. If we round up to two hours, that’s $1700 an hour for a lousy caulk job.

At this point all I can do is offer that this activity is borderline criminal and that you avoid these guys at all costs. 2 stars because my home actually is warmer. I just could have done it for $20 of caulk and 2 hours of my own time.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCpwncgePV68_PJRzsi0INb&fid=0x0:0x76dcba4db9e18597"],"When":"2025-12-22"},{"Name":"Harris Huang","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKIQx4vB-E4WKP1TxJTA0YYZYGOZ_TO3b7ZAPHIUo4ghq-9jQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Hired Window Repair too replace 3 doors into glass doors.

The technicians skillfully took our old doors and frames apart then put in the new glass doors.

They cleaned up after themselves and fixed the frames.

Their IT team also quickly set up our fob scanning system with very little trouble.

And finally double checked all the doors making sure it opens and closes with no issues

Tenants in my 16 stories residential building loves how they look.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDX_Os_xxh7sRoU-_3bWb_3&fid=0x0:0x76dcba4db9e18597"],"When":"2025-7-8"},{"Name":"Vicente Malave","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXMe_NALrJwydNYcXpAg5ir0aiqVKtZTYttGeLrAKqcOL2fyRHh=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The team at Window Repair NYC was contracted to repair problematic windows and damaged screens in our coop building.  Their staff have been professional all the way.  They were flexible with work days and even conducted complementary site surveys to provide accurate quotes.  We were able to arrive at pricing that accomodated our budget to complete the work needed.
Our 20yr old alumnium windows are now able to open and close much easier. Several of our windows had child safety bars which they removed and reinstalled at no additional cost. They were also able to remove the original window screens to replace damaged mesh with better material.  This was something that other contractors stated was not possible.
If you have windows issues look no further.  Window Repair NYC are knowledgeable
and will work with you to address your problems.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCwlr_22wE&fid=0x0:0x76dcba4db9e18597"],"When":"2025-3-18"},{"Name":"Kristina Mortimer","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKhj7f-vxM6ciOQyUgRaKw6T7p1L4kAueb3tPfdkDRacbZk2Es=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"The window repair itself was done well, but the experience was extremely unprofessional.

I had an appointment scheduled for 12–1 PM. No one showed up, and I received no update. I had to reach out at 1 PM, only to be told the technician was still 30–40 minutes away, nearly two hours late.

After the repair, I was pressured aggressively for payment while I was leading a previously scheduled 2:30 PM work meeting - the entire reason I booked the earlier time slot. I received multiple calls and texts insisting I “needed to pay,” and was even told I “clearly did not pay” after I had already sent proof of payment.

Additionally, a smoked cigarette was left on my building stairs, and the technicians remained parked on my street afterward, which felt uncomfortable given the payment pressure.

Customer service later apologized, but this level of poor communication and professionalism is unacceptable. I would not use this company again.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rich H","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX6069WMSE9dMebuX1HO1fV9_blxRAqaFdpA8VqlAaNbe4xg1Pw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"I strongly advise against using this window installation company.

They were hired to replace four double-glass panes in existing window frames due to cracked weatherstripping and fogged glass in 40-year-old windows. Instead of properly replacing the damaged weatherstripping, they took shortcuts and relied heavily on sealant, which was applied sloppily and left an unprofessional appearance.

One of the four new glass panes was broken during transit while being delivered, and this was not disclosed to me. I only discovered it when I noticed one pane was still the original glass and caught the crew packing up to leave. The supervisor I spoke with by phone promised a chargeback for the broken pane, but weeks later nothing was returned and there was no follow-up.

They were quick to collect full payment but dragged their feet on returning money that should have been charged back. Even with all the sealant used, the windows are still just as drafty as before.

While they did offer warranty service to address the issues, given the poor workmanship and lack of transparency, I declined. I would not recommend this company.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marty Martinez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIWRk3bcw6j6FNOqQfElTc-_kc2mdP3LrMdMj1Dw5dYO0UOHA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Window Repair  of NYC is an excellent company with superb workers who  are honest, and hardworking. Look no further.
